title: Generate a .lib linked with Vulkan and GLM
snippet: Coucou !Ceci est un test!#pragma once#include &lt;vulkan/vulkan.hpp&gt;#include &lt;glm/matrix.hpp&gt;struct Component { glm::mat4 model;};namespace ARE{class Context{private: VkAllocationCallbacks* allocator = nullptr; VkInstance instance;public: Context(); ~Context();};}#include "Context.hpp"#include &lt;iostream&gt;namespace ARE{Context::Context(){ VkInstanceCreateInfo createInfo{}; VkResult result = vkCreateInstance(&amp;createInfo, allocator, &amp;instance); if (result == VK_SUCCESS) { std::cout &lt;&lt; "VKSuccess" &lt;&lt; std::endl; } else { std::cout &lt;&lt; "VKFailure" &lt;&lt; std::endl; }}Context::~Context(){ std::cout &lt;&lt; "vkdestroy" &lt;&lt; std::endl; vkDestroyInstance(instance, allocator);}}

title: Git : Everything you have to know
snippet: Git environnmentWhat is git?Git is a program.You can use it if you want to save files on a remote server, similar to google drive and the cloud.The reasons why git is loved by programmers are : Because it keeps a version of old files.If a crash suddenly occurs in your code, you can just go back to a previous version, when it worked. Because you can merge files.if multiple programmers work on the same file, they can fuse their changes easily instead of keeping only one version.No matter the remote server or the git interface used, the commands will be the same, because they all use git.What is Github?Github is a remote server, where you can store your files using git.It provides free storage and some useful tools, like: Github Action, which can be used to automatically run tests on different os Github Pages, that provides a free internet websiteThere are other providers, like: Gitlab BitbucketWhat is a Git Interface?By default, you have to use command lines to use git.However, there is something called “git interfaces”. They provide an easy to use visual interface to use git instead of raw commands.Examples of git interfaces: Github Desktop Sourcetree GitkrakenRemote servers do not require a specific git interface.You can use Github Desktop with Gitlab, and SourceTree with Github.Clone a repositoryWhat is a repository?Using an accountUsing ssh keysGit commandsBasicsgit addgit commit -m “”git pushgit statusRetrieve filesgit log : see the history of commitsgit checkout : go to previous commitsgit checkout : retrieve a file from a specific commitWork in a teaamgit pullgit branch git checkout -bgit merge.gitignore

title: Exercises for all languages
snippet: For / whileThe following exercises only require a loop (for or while), print(), and variables.They don’t require anything else, like if statements, functions, arrays, etc. Display all numbers from 1 to 100 Display all numbers from 1 to 100 and then display “Finished” Display all numbers from 1 to 100, but skipping 1 out of 2 numbers Display all numbers from 100 to 1 (compared to before, it’s in the reverse order) Display 100, then 1, then 99, then 2, then 98, then 3, etc until you display 1 then 100 Display all numbers from 1 to 10 on the same line, separated by a tabulation Display all numbers from 1 to 10 on the same line, separated by a tabulation, all of that 10 times (on 10 different lines) Display an addition table Display a multiplication table IfData structures (with classes) Make a stack Make a queue Make a forward linked list Make a double linked list Make a binary tree Make a binary search tree Make a graph Make a hash table

title: Vulkan Introduction
snippet: What is Vulkan?Vulkan is a rendering api. With it, you can render things, or compute data on the GPU.Why Vulkan?The advantages of Vulkan are : Performance (better than DirectX11 and OpenGL) Cross plateform compatibility (which is not the case for DirectX and Metal)This makes Vulkan one of the best rendering api available in the market, if not the best.Why this tutorial?There are already some tutorials on Vulkan, but I think they are too much theoretical, and only explain concepts.However, without a good concrete example, it is still hard to grasp Vulkan.How?The goal of this tutorial is to make a Rendering Library, to render things easily.It will show you a concrete example of a good code architecture with Vulkan.That means you’ll see how Vulkan works, but also the dependencies between each Vulkan Object, that is too often forgotten on other tutorials.
